description: Reward system built from scratch for Embral. Ages 4-8. Neurodivergent-first. Family-centered.
design_laws: Effort is always rewarded. Perfection is never required.
No real-money purchases of any kind.
Competition is local and optional — never against strangers.
Absence is never punished. Sick days and missed days erase nothing.
Family time is mechanically irreplaceable — no shortcut replaces it.
Hardware adds depth. Hardware never locks content.
Every reward reinforces a developmental domain, not arbitrary grind.

gold.name: Gold
gold.description: Earned through quests, exploration, and daily play. Spent on cosmetics, house items, pet accessories, and NPC home requests.

elemental_essence.name: Elemental Essence
elemental_essence.description: Rare material dropped in dungeons and from Embral's Trials. Used to evolve Wisps and craft special items.

play_points.name: Play Points
play_points.description: Earned from local mini-games. Player can play against his companion (AI) or with up to 3 family members. Spent at the Play Shop for exclusive items.

earn_rates.notes: Participation always earns something. Winning earns more. No one goes home empty.

raid_points.name: Raid Points
raid_points.description: Earned exclusively through nightly review sessions with a parent. 3 in one week unlocks the Weekend Family Raid.

level_formula.description: XP needed scales with level. Early levels fast to reward the tutorial. Later levels take longer to create satisfaction arcs.

1.unlock: Tutorial complete — Hearthveil open, Ignavarr accessible

5.unlock: Wisps unlocked — first Wisp gifted by Ignavarr

10.unlock: Companions unlocked — Companion Registry in Hearthveil opens

25.unlock: Ground mounts unlocked. Companion home capacity +1.

50.unlock: New content layer across all realms. Companion home capacity +1.

75.unlock: New content layer across all realms. Companion home capacity +1.

pet_happiness_maxed.notes: When any pet reaches max happiness

npc_mastery.description: Every teacher NPC has a personal mastery level for each player. Tracks learning progress independently per NPC. Unlocks harder quest tiers as child demonstrates mastery.
npc_mastery.how_it_works: Quest completion records a score (0.0 to 1.0). Average of the last 5 scores is calculated. If average is 0.8 or above, mastery level increases by 1.

level_benefits.1: Base quests available. NPC greets normally.
level_benefits.2: Harder quest variants appear. NPC acknowledges Player's growth.
level_benefits.3: NPC shares personal lore and backstory. Bonus dialogue unlocks.
level_benefits.4: Advanced quest tier unlocks. NPC relationship feels deep and personal.
level_benefits.5: Mastery reward granted. Unique item or pet exclusive to this NPC. NPC calls Player by a special title.

mastery_reward_types: Unique pet species only available from this NPC at mastery 5
Special house decoration tied to this NPC's visual theme
Companion outfit exclusive to this NPC's realm
Permanent +10% XP multiplier for this NPC's quest category

xp_multipliers.notes: Multipliers stack. First-time + mastery 5 + no hints = 3.75x. Feels earned, not inflated.

special_quest_drops.npc_request_complete: House decoration or cosmetic unique to that NPC

nightly_review.description: Josh and Player review the day together through the game's Review Portal in Hearthveil. Ignavarr hosts. Short, calm, bonding-first. Not a quiz.
nightly_review.access: Parent opens Review Portal from Hearthveil. Evening or bedtime recommended.
questions: What was one thing that felt good today?
Was there anything that felt hard or tricky?
Did you help someone, or did someone help you?
What's one thing you want to try or learn tomorrow?
nightly_review.question_format: Visual icons accompany each question. Player can tap an image, speak, or have parent read aloud. No wrong answers. Parent guides.

raid_point_system.how_awarded: Parent taps the Raid Point orb when a meaningful moment happens. Parent judgment only. Never automated.
qualifying_moments: Player named a feeling without being prompted
Player described a moment of trying again after something was hard
Player mentioned helping someone or an act of kindness
Player expressed curiosity or asked a question about the world
Player acknowledged something difficult without shutting down
A genuine moment of connection or shared laughter happened
raid_point_system.philosophy: These moments cannot be gamed or rushed. They happen or they don't. The system trusts the parent.

weekly_review.description: Optional longer session on Sunday. Player and parent look back at the whole week's quests, achievements, and moments together in-game.
weekly_review.bonus: Automatically awards 2 Raid Points on completion. No threshold moment needed.
weekly_review.format: Same four questions but expanded. Ignavarr shows the week's highlights as a short storybook.

rewards.notes: Small reward for doing the review. Same every time. Player knows it's coming.

weekend_family_raid.description: Unlocked when Player earns 3+ Raid Points in a week. Triggered Saturday or Sunday — family chooses when.
weekend_family_raid.unlock_notification: Friday evening: Ignavarr delivers a message — 'A great challenge stirs in the realm. Return this weekend to face it together.'
weekend_family_raid.core_rule: At least one parent must participate alongside Player. This is a together activity, not a solo challenge.

knowledge_raid.name: The Scholar's Challenge
knowledge_raid.description: Trivia and learning game. Parent and Player on the same team. Ignavarr asks questions mixing the week's quests with fun family topics.
knowledge_raid.educational_tie: Spaced repetition — revisits the week's learning domains in a celebration context

physical_raid.name: The Forge Trial
physical_raid.description: Physical activity challenge. Build an obstacle course together. Complete it together.
physical_raid.hardware_active: Wii balance board tracks balance sections. PS5 motion tracks throwing/catching sections.
physical_raid.hardware_fallback: Floor-is-lava, freeze dance, or any physical family game. Same reward.
physical_raid.educational_tie: Gross motor integration, coordination, parent-child co-regulation through movement

creative_raid.name: The Drift Gauntlet
creative_raid.description: Creative challenge. Draw, build, or create something together matching Ignavarr's prompt.
creative_raid.educational_tie: Fine motor, creative expression, collaborative imagination

card_game_raid.name: The Vault Duel
card_game_raid.description: Play a real physical card game together. Can use printable Embral-themed cards or any household card game.
card_game_raid.educational_tie: Number recognition, pattern matching, turn-taking, strategic thinking, graceful losing

cooking_raid.name: The Rootstead Feast
cooking_raid.description: Prepare or cook something together as a family. Player measures, pours, mixes. Parent supervises and participates.
cooking_raid.educational_tie: Life skills, measurement and math, nutrition awareness, multi-step sequencing

story_raid.name: The Tidemark Epic
story_raid.description: Collaborative story. Ignavarr gives the opening line. Player and parent alternate sentences. Ends with an illustrated page for the Trophy Room.
story_raid.educational_tie: Narrative structure, vocabulary, listening, expressive language

nature_raid.name: The Spire Expedition
nature_raid.description: Outdoor observation challenge. Find, count, and document natural things. Documented in an in-game adventure journal.
nature_raid.weather_fallback: Indoor version: observe things through a window, use nature books, or watch a nature documentary together.
nature_raid.educational_tie: Scientific observation, counting, nature literacy, curiosity

failure_acceptance_raid.name: The Drift's Falling Challenge
failure_acceptance_raid.description: A challenge intentionally designed to fail. Multiple collapses are expected and celebrated. The fail is the lesson.
failure_acceptance_raid.example: Build the tallest tower possible from household items. It WILL fall. Race to rebuild. Ignavarr celebrates every collapse: 'MAGNIFICENT! Do it again!'
failure_acceptance_raid.educational_tie: Growth mindset, persistence, frustration tolerance, flexible thinking
failure_acceptance_raid.special_mechanic: Ignavarr has unique voice lines ONLY triggered by the tower falling. The collapse literally unlocks new content. Failure is mechanically rewarded.

boss_encounter.description: At the climax of the Family Raid, Ignavarr presents the final challenge. Completing it defeats the Raid Boss.
boss_encounter.format: One final task tied to the raid type. Completing it together = victory.
boss_encounter.attempts: Unlimited. No penalty for trying again.
boss_encounter.failure_dialogue: Ignavarr: 'That was incredible. You almost had it. One more time?' Tone is always warm, never disappointed.

family_raid_shop.description: Exclusive shop. Only accessible after completing a Weekend Family Raid. Items cannot be obtained any other way. Rotates each raid.
family_raid_shop.rotation: Random draw from pool. Some items are rarer than others.
family_raid_shop.currency: Gold earned during the raid session

items.name: Ignavarr's Ember Lantern
items.description: A small floating flame that drifts through Player's room in-game. Only Ignavarr can give these.

items.name: Family Crest Banner
items.description: A customizable banner for Player's House. Grows more ornate with each raid won.

items.name: Raid Wisp Egg
items.description: A rare Wisp species only born from Family Raids. Cannot be found in dungeons.

items.name: Memory Crystal Frame
items.description: Displays in the Player's House Trophy Room. Each one records a raid completed.

items.name: Raid Hero's Outfit
items.description: Exclusive outfit for companion. Marked with a small Family Crest badge.

items.name: Realm Path Scroll
items.description: Unlocks a hidden scenic path in one realm. Changes each raid. Not a quest area — just a beautiful secret place.

items.name: Bond Ribbon
items.description: Can evolve a Wisp with maximum bond regardless of essence requirement. Rare.

family_raid_shop.exclusive_marker: All items display a small Family Crest icon showing they came from a Family Raid

mini_games.description: Local multiplayer games. Player vs AI companion (default) or up to 4 human family players on the same screen.

hardware_support.base: Keyboard, mouse, or any standard gamepad
hardware_support.ps5_dualsense: Haptic feedback on key moments, LED pulses with player color

academic_integration.description: Some mini-games are flagged as academically tied to active quests. Completing them counts toward quest progress — play and learn are the same action.
academic_integration.non_academic_games: Still earn Play Points and are always available. Pure fun is valid.

catalogue.name: Wisp Match
catalogue.description: Memory card-matching game using Wisp portraits. Find pairs before the other player.

catalogue.name: Word Catch
catalogue.description: Words rain from the sky. Catch only the words that rhyme with the target word.

catalogue.name: Number Race
catalogue.description: An equation appears. First player to tap the correct answer wins the round.

catalogue.name: Feeling Sort
catalogue.description: Character faces appear showing different emotions. Sort them into the correct feeling jars.

catalogue.name: The Balance Trial
catalogue.description: Hold still on the Wii balance board. Whoever stays most centered longest wins.
catalogue.hardware_fallback: Stand on one foot. Parent times it. Beats it? Win.
catalogue.academic_skill: Gross motor balance and body awareness

catalogue.name: Letter Bounce
catalogue.description: Letters bounce around the screen. Collect the letters to spell the word shown at the top.

embral_trials.description: One per realm. Major milestone encounters. Hardware-first design with complete base fallback. Ignavarr narrates each as a legendary event.
embral_trials.unlock_condition: Complete all 3 starter NPCs in the realm
embral_trials.philosophy: Embral's Trials are the physical and mental celebration of realm mastery. They should feel like an event, not just a battle.

hardware_integration.wii_balance_board: Lean-based mechanics — hold stance, dodge by leaning, charge by holding center
hardware_integration.wii_wiimote: Swing and point mechanics — aim actions, swipe to block, gesture abilities
hardware_integration.wii_ir_camera: Precision targeting for puzzle-based trial sections
hardware_integration.ps5_dualsense: Haptic impact feedback, adaptive trigger weight for effort mechanics, LED realm color
hardware_integration.base_fallback: Turn-based keyboard version — same educational content, same rewards, same story

ember_hollow_trial.name: The Heart Trial
ember_hollow_trial.description: A being made of reflected emotions. To weaken it, Player must correctly identify the emotions it projects.
ember_hollow_trial.mechanic_base: Multiple choice emotion identification. Wrong answers strengthen the Guardian. Correct answers weaken it.
ember_hollow_trial.mechanic_wii: Balance board sways reflect the Guardian's emotional instability. Hold center = Guardian weakens faster.
ember_hollow_trial.mechanic_ps5: Haptic pulses match the Guardian's heartbeat. Feel when it's about to attack.
ember_hollow_trial.educational_tie: Emotion regulation and identification under gentle pressure

tidemark_trial.name: The Word Surge
tidemark_trial.description: A creature of living language. Build a path across the water by spelling words correctly.
tidemark_trial.mechanic_base: Spell words using on-screen keyboard. Each correct word builds a bridge section.
tidemark_trial.mechanic_wii: Wiimote swings place letter blocks on the bridge. Aim with IR camera for bonus sections.
tidemark_trial.mechanic_ps5: Haptic confirms each correct letter placed.
tidemark_trial.educational_tie: Spelling and phonics applied under time and challenge

forge_run_trial.name: The Forge Gauntlet
forge_run_trial.description: A full physical encounter. The most hardware-active trial in the game.
forge_run_trial.mechanic_base: Precision platformer section with timing-based challenges.
forge_run_trial.mechanic_wii: Balance board + wiimote + nunchuck combined. Balance to hold ground. Swing to strike. Move to dodge.
forge_run_trial.mechanic_ps5: Motion dodge rolls. Adaptive trigger weight for blocking. Haptic on every impact.
forge_run_trial.notes: This is the hardware showcase trial. If only one trial uses all hardware simultaneously, it is this one.
forge_run_trial.educational_tie: Gross motor integration, coordination, spatial awareness, persistence through failure

rootstead_trial.name: The Life Lodge Trial
rootstead_trial.description: The Elder tests whether Player has truly learned to care for the Lodge. Complete a multi-step life skill sequence correctly.
rootstead_trial.mechanic_base: Order-of-operations puzzle. Place the steps in the correct sequence.
rootstead_trial.mechanic_wii: Gesture-based actions match each life skill step. Wipe, fold, pour, measure.
rootstead_trial.mechanic_ps5: Haptic confirms correct sequence steps. Vibrates distinctly on errors.
rootstead_trial.educational_tie: Life skills sequencing, independence, following multi-step directions

the_spire_trial.name: The Vault Challenge
the_spire_trial.description: A being of pure logic. It presents math and science challenges that grow harder as Player succeeds.
the_spire_trial.mechanic_base: Math and logic puzzles with visual scaffolding. Difficulty adapts based on NPC mastery level.
the_spire_trial.mechanic_ps5: Haptic pulses on correct answers. Adaptive triggers add weight to 'thinking' moments.
the_spire_trial.educational_tie: Math, logic, cause-and-effect, problem solving under adaptive challenge

the_drift_trial.name: The Creation Crucible
the_drift_trial.description: The Muse challenges Player to create something matching a feeling or theme — before the timer runs.
the_drift_trial.mechanic_base: Drawing or selection-based creative challenge. Choose elements that match the theme.
the_drift_trial.mechanic_wii: Motion drawing with Wiimote. Draw in the air to create.
the_drift_trial.educational_tie: Creative expression, fine motor, emotional vocabulary through art

first_win.realm_seal_display: Shown in Player's House Trophy Room. One per realm.
first_win.unique_item: Cosmetic or wisp accessory unique to this trial

mini_boss.description: Stronger Wisp or elemental creature. Appears 1-2 times per dungeon run at random positions.

dungeon_boss.description: End-of-dungeon boss. Multi-phase. Phase 1 is battle. Phase 2 adds a puzzle layer.

first_clear_rewards.unique_cosmetic: One cosmetic tied to this dungeon's visual theme

pet_system.description: Pets live exclusively at Player's House. They have a mini-game interaction area. They give passive bonuses to the main game based on their happiness level.
pet_system.travel: Pets never travel with the player. They stay home.
pet_system.battle: Pets never battle. That is the Wisp system.

happiness.decay: Loses 1 point per in-game day if not visited. Never drops below 20.

passive_bonuses.60_to_79: +5% gold and +3% XP on all quests
passive_bonuses.80_to_100: +8% gold, +5% XP, and +3% chance of rare item find in dungeons

visiting_reward.notes: Small daily reward for visiting and interacting with pets. Encourages the bond.

wisp_evolution.description: Wisps grow through three visual tiers. Evolution requires reaching level 30 and spending matching essence.
wisp_evolution.tier_3_overworld_passive: Tier 3 Wisp leaves a subtle element trail while following player in overworld. Cosmetic only.

play_shop.description: Items only available with Play Points. Earned through mini-games. Cannot be bought.

cosmetic_shop.description: Purchased with Gold only. No real money. No power items. Cosmetic only.

daily_login.philosophy: Rewards showing up. Never punishes absence. No streak mechanics that break.
daily_login.after_day_7: Resets to day 1. No penalty for gaps in play. The schedule picks up from where Player left off if absent.

emotional_checkin_reward.critical_design_note: Reward is IDENTICAL regardless of what mood Player reports. There is no incentive to report a happy mood to get a bigger reward. This is essential — the check-in must feel safe and honest.

ascension.description: Triggered at level 100. Not a reset. A full content expansion.
ascension.what_is_kept: Everything. All pets, Wisps, companions, gold, house items, NPC relationships, mastery levels, trophy room.

what_changes.world: All realms gain new visual layers — deeper areas, new biome sections visible that were previously locked.
what_changes.quests: New NPC quest tiers available across all realms. Harder content but same scaffolding support.
what_changes.npc_dialogue: All NPCs have new dialogue trees acknowledging Player's ascension. They remember his journey.
what_changes.ignavarr: New story chapter from Ignavarr unlocks. The narrative advances meaningfully.
what_changes.flying_mounts: Flying mounts unlocked. New way to navigate the overworld.
what_changes.visual: Player character gains a subtle ascension aura effect — cosmetic, not pay-to-show.

ascension.subsequent_ascensions: Every 100 levels after first ascension adds another content expansion. No defined cap. The game grows with the child.

what_embral_does_not_have.no_season_passes: No paid battle passes. No FOMO. No time-limited exclusive content behind a paywall.
what_embral_does_not_have.no_catch_all_items: No item that automatically captures any Wisp. Earning a capture is part of the experience.
what_embral_does_not_have.no_online_pvp: No competition against unknown players. Local family multiplayer only.
what_embral_does_not_have.no_real_money_power: Gold, essence, and Play Points cannot be purchased. All earned through play.
what_embral_does_not_have.no_streak_punishment: Missing days never destroys progress. The game waits for Player.
what_embral_does_not_have.no_generated_quests: No AI generates quest content at runtime. Every quest is hand-authored with research basis.
what_embral_does_not_have.no_negative_feedback_loop: No mechanic that makes doing poorly feel like losing something. Try again is always free.
